hypothesis: If an EGNN-based diffusion model (AMDEN) with energy-based denoising, VE/VP noise schedules, ghost-atom density control, and HMC refinement is trained on amorphous MD data and optionally conditioned on Young’s modulus, then it will generate equilibrated, density-controlled amorphous structures whose RDFs, bond-angle distributions, structure factors, and energy distributions match those of MD-quenched references and whose moduli follow the conditioning, because energy-aware scores and physics-based refinement preserve realistic local environments while classifier-free guidance steers global mechanical response.
